title: elaeocarpus gitingensis
intro: Elaeocarpus gitingensis, a species of flowering plant, is a type of tree native to the Philippines, specifically found in the island of Panay. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Elaeocarpus gitingensis include reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of the plant can be used to make teas, infusions, or decoctions, which are often consumed to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
